# Veryfront CLI Documentation Summary

## Overview
Veryfront CLI is a zero-config command-line interface for building and deploying AI-native React applications. It provides commands for project initialization, development, building, and remote synchronization with git-like workflows.

## Commands Reference

### init - Initialize Project

```bash
veryfront init [project-name] [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-t, --template <name>`: Project template (ai, app, blog, docs, minimal) - default: ai
- `--integrations <list>`: Service integrations (gmail,slack,github,calendar)
- `-c, --config <file>`: JSON config file for programmatic scaffolding
- `--skip-install`: Skip automatic dependency installation
- `--skip-env-prompt`: Skip environment variable prompts

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront init                                    # Interactive wizard
veryfront init my-app                             # Named project
veryfront init my-agent --template ai             # AI template
veryfront init my-agent --integrations gmail,slack
veryfront init --config project.json              # From config file
```

### dev - Development Server

```bash
veryfront dev [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-p, --port <number>`: Port to run on (default: 3000)
- `--no-hmr`: Disable hot module replacement
- `--open`: Open browser automatically

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront dev
veryfront dev --port 8080
veryfront dev --open
veryfront dev --no-hmr
```

### build - Production Build

```bash
veryfront build [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-o, --output <dir>`: Output directory (default: .veryfront/output)
- `--no-compress`: Disable compression
- `--no-split`: Disable code splitting
- `--no-ssg`: Disable static generation
- `--include <paths>`: Include specific paths in SSG
- `--exclude <paths>`: Exclude paths from SSG
- `--dry-run`: Preview what will be built
- `--preset <name>`: Select build preset (e.g., embedded)

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront build
veryfront build --output dist
veryfront build --no-ssg
veryfront build --preset embedded
veryfront build --include /docs --exclude /api
veryfront build --dry-run
```

### serve / preview - Production Server

```bash
veryfront serve [options]
veryfront preview [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-p, --port <number>`: Port to run on (default: 3000)
- `--hostname <host>`: Hostname to bind to (default: 0.0.0.0)

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront serve
veryfront serve --port 8080
VERYFRONT_USE_REDIS_CACHE=1 veryfront serve
```

### pull - Download Project Files

```bash
veryfront pull [options]
```

**Options:**
- `--projects <slugs>`: Comma-separated list of project slugs
- `-d, --dir <path>`: Target directory (default: .)
- `-b, --branch <name>`: Branch to pull from (default: main)
- `--env <name>`: Environment to pull from (e.g., production, staging)
- `--release <version>`: Release version to pull from (e.g., v1.2.0)
- `-f, --force`: Force overwrite without confirmation
- `--dry-run`: Show what would be written

**Priority:** --env > --release > --branch > main

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront pull
veryfront pull --dir ./my-project
veryfront pull --branch feature-header
veryfront pull --env production
veryfront pull --release v1.2.0
veryfront pull --projects project-a,project-b,project-c
veryfront pull --dry-run
veryfront pull --force
```

### push - Upload Local Changes

```bash
veryfront push [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-d, --dir <path>`: Source directory (default: .)
- `-b, --branch <name>`: Branch name (auto-generated if omitted, use 'main' for direct push)
- `-f, --force`: Push without confirmation
- `--dry-run`: Show what would be uploaded

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront push                          # Auto-generated branch
veryfront push --dir ./my-project
veryfront push --branch feature-header
veryfront push --branch main            # Push directly to main
veryfront push --dry-run
```

### merge - Merge Branches

```bash
veryfront merge <branch> [options]
```

**Options:**
- `--into <branch>`: Target branch to merge into (default: main)
- `-f, --force`: Merge without confirmation
- `--dry-run`: Preview merge without executing

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront merge feature-login
veryfront merge hotfix --into staging
veryfront merge feature-header --dry-run
```

### deploy - Create Release and Deploy

```bash
veryfront deploy [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-b, --branch <name>`: Branch to release from (default: main)
- `--env <name>`: Environment to deploy to (default: production)
- `--release-name <name>`: Custom release name (auto-generated if omitted)
- `-f, --force`: Deploy without confirmation
- `--dry-run`: Preview without executing

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront deploy
veryfront deploy --env staging
veryfront deploy --branch feature-x --env preview
veryfront deploy --release-name v1.2.0
veryfront deploy --dry-run
```

### doctor - Health Check

```bash
veryfront doctor [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-s, --strict`: Treat warnings as errors

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront doctor
veryfront doctor --strict
```

### clean - Clean Artifacts

```bash
veryfront clean [options]
```

**Options:**
- `--cache`: Clean cache only
- `--build`: Clean build output only
- `--all`: Clean everything (node_modules, .deno, .veryfront)
- `-f, --force`: Skip confirmation prompts

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront clean
veryfront clean --cache
veryfront clean --all
veryfront clean --all --force
```

### routes - List Routes

```bash
veryfront routes [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-j, --json`: Output as JSON

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront routes
veryfront routes --json
```

### lock - Manage Lockfile

```bash
veryfront lock [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-l, --list`: List all locked imports
- `-u, --update`: Update all locked imports
- `--verify`: Verify integrity of locked imports
- `--clear`: Clear the lockfile
- `-f, --force`: Skip confirmation prompts

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront lock               # List locked imports
veryfront lock --list
veryfront lock --verify      # Check integrity
veryfront lock --update      # Refresh all entries
veryfront lock --clear       # Remove lockfile
```

### analyze-chunks - Bundle Analysis

```bash
veryfront analyze-chunks [options]
```

**Options:**
- `-o, --output <file>`: Output analysis to file

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront analyze-chunks
veryfront analyze-chunks --output bundle-analysis.json
```

### generate - Code Scaffolding

```bash
veryfront generate <type> [name]
veryfront g <type> [name]
```

**Types:**
- `page`: Generate a new page
- `layout`: Generate a new layout
- `provider`: Generate a context provider
- `api`: Generate an API endpoint
- `integration`: Generate a service integration

**Examples:**
```bash
veryfront generate page about
veryfront generate layout admin
veryfront generate api users/[id]
veryfront generate provider auth
veryfront generate integration              # Interactive wizard
veryfront generate integration twilio       # With name preset
```

## Configuration

### .veryfrontrc

Project configuration for remote synchronization:

```json
{
  "projectSlug": "my-project",
  "apiUrl": "https://api.veryfront.com",
  "apiToken": "vf_...",
  "projects": ["project-a", "project-b"]
}
```

### veryfront.config.ts

Framework configuration:

```typescript
export default {
  fs: {
    type: "veryfront-api",
    veryfront: {
      baseUrl: "https://api.veryfront.com",
      proxyMode: false,
      cache: { enabled: true, ttl: 60000 }
    }
  },
  dev: {
    port: 3000,
    hmr: true
  },
  build: {
    compress: true,
    split: true,
    ssg: true
  }
}
```

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `VERYFRONT_API_TOKEN` | API authentication token |
| `VERYFRONT_PROJECT_SLUG` | Default project slug |
| `PROXY_MODE` | Enable proxy mode (0 or 1) |
| `PRODUCTION_MODE` | Use production releases (0 or 1) |
| `NO_COLOR` | Disable colored output |

## AI Application Development

Veryfront provides built-in support for AI agents with MCP integration:

```typescript
import { createAgent, defineTool } from "veryfront/ai";

const agent = createAgent({
  model: "claude-3-opus",
  tools: [searchTool, calendarTool],
  memory: true,
});

const searchTool = defineTool({
  name: "search",
  description: "Search the web",
  parameters: z.object({
    query: z.string(),
  }),
  execute: async ({ query }) => {
    // Implementation
  },
});
```

## Project Structure

```
my-ai-app/
├── pages/
│   ├── index.tsx          # Main page
│   └── chat/
│       └── index.tsx      # Chat interface
├── api/
│   ├── chat.ts            # Chat API endpoint
│   └── webhooks/
│       └── slack.ts       # Slack webhook handler
├── agents/
│   ├── assistant.ts       # Main assistant agent
│   └── tools/
│       ├── search.ts      # Search tool
│       └── calendar.ts    # Calendar tool
├── workflows/
│   └── onboarding.ts      # Onboarding workflow
├── providers/
│   └── auth.tsx           # Auth provider
├── veryfront.config.ts    # Framework config
└── .veryfrontrc           # Remote sync config
```

## Workflow Example

```bash
# 1. Initialize project
veryfront init my-agent --template ai --integrations gmail,slack

# 2. Start development
cd my-agent && veryfront dev

# 3. Pull latest from remote
veryfront pull

# 4. Make changes locally, then push
veryfront push --branch feature-login

# 5. Merge branch into main
veryfront merge feature-login

# 6. Deploy to staging first
veryfront deploy --env staging

# 7. Verify staging, then deploy to production
veryfront deploy
```

## Module Architecture

The CLI is organized with clear module boundaries:

```
src/cli/
├── commands/              # Individual command implementations
│   ├── pull.ts           # Pull command + PullSource types
│   ├── pull.test.ts      # Co-located unit tests
│   ├── pull.integration.ts # Integration tests (real API)
│   ├── push.ts           # Push command
│   ├── merge.ts          # Merge command
│   ├── deploy.ts         # Deploy command
│   ├── clean.ts          # Clean command
│   ├── dev.ts            # Dev server command
│   ├── build/            # Build command modules
│   ├── init/             # Init command modules
│   ├── doctor/           # Doctor command modules
│   └── generate/         # Generate command modules
├── help/                 # Help system
│   └── command-definitions.ts  # Central COMMANDS registry
├── index/                # Entry points and routing
│   ├── command-router.ts # Command dispatch
│   ├── arg-parser.ts     # CLI argument parsing
│   └── types.ts          # Shared types
├── shared/               # Shared utilities
│   └── config.ts         # API client, config resolution
├── utils/                # CLI utilities
│   ├── index.ts          # Spinner, prompts, logging
│   └── terminal-select.ts
└── templates/            # Project scaffolding templates
```

**Module Boundaries:**
- Commands export: `Command function`, `Schema`, `parseArgs`, helper functions
- Shared exports: `ApiClient`, `createApiClient`, `resolveConfig`
- Utils export: `createSpinner`, `confirmPrompt`, `logSuccess/Error/Info`

## CLI Development Guidelines

- Use Zod schemas for CLI argument validation
- Export parseArgs and schema from command modules for testability
- Always provide --dry-run flag for destructive operations
- Use central COMMANDS registry for help and usage strings
- Fail fast with clear error messages - no silent fallbacks
- Co-locate unit tests with implementation files
